raw_consumer_statement:
I would like to file a complaint on Citi regarding their XXXX XXXX Credit Card. I filed a complaint ( dispute ) with Citi regarding a fraudulent charge on XX/XX/year>. Citi reached out to the merchant on XX/XX/year>, and the merchant responded on XX/XX/year>. Citi then closed the investigation and considered the merchants response as facts without offering me a chance for rebuttal or providing me with the evidence from the merchant. My major concern is that this merchant is a complete scam and fraud and misrepresented themselves to be XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX, when they were not. I am a senior citizen, and it is not easy for me to recognize scam practices. My entire basis of this complaint was because I called a company thinking they were XXXX, asked them if they were XXXX, and they responded yes. I proceeded to make a car rental reservation and only provided my credit card information because I was told this was XXXX. Once I received the email confirmation and forwarded to my daughter, she realized it looked like a scam. She noticed typos and the senders email address did not say XXXX. At this point I proceeded to report to my credit card company ( Citi ) and file a dispute. My daughter called the real XXXX company, and they confirmed with us both on the phone there was not a reservation in my name. They also confirmed the confirmation number I was given by this imposter company was not a real XXXX confirmation number. At this point my daughter reserved me a car rental with the real XXXX company. 
I provided Citi with the email confirmation from the scam company and my receipt from the real XXXX company. Unfortunately, Citi closed the investigation and found me liable for this charge. I have called multiple times to speak with a manager and after being transferred to 6 or 7 people, no one is willing to help. Citi also never gave me the opportunity to dispute their decision. There should be a better way of handling a billing or fraud case when a company misrepresents themselves and poses as another company.
